channel.title: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "{Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn" - Extradimensional beings like this one see themselves more as expendable than blessed.

item.title: Re: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn
item.content:encoded: Thanks again, I've cut more extraneous features previously presented in the concept to focus more on describing the emotional narrative. The entity's electronic-jamming and schizophrenic properties can be explored more in the drafting stage. I also removed any direct mention of other SCP's to mitigate potential crosslink complications.

item.title: Re: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn
item.content:encoded:
Hello, let's get down to it.

+ show block
– hide block

The creature's origins here are fascinating. I myself have been working on a group of inter-dimensional journalists called the Wandsmen with similar goals to your "Frequency", though they have more broad methods.

So, a lot of this concept will land on the emotional weight of your skip being functionally a slave to this entity and very aware that it is utterly disposable.

I admit, crossovers with Series 1 skips are often frowned upon, so I might see if there are some more modern or original anomalies you could replace 187 with.

Honestly so far though I like this a lot, though you probably want to tip your hand as to a few more details about the Frequency early on, so the concept can be examined and iterated upon.

Also, you might want to try reading the concept aloud. I know that's odd, but even at the concept stage readability is a big deal for getting greenlit and there were some sections of this, especially in the description that left me confused.

Overall I think this idea has legs and I look forward to seeing later versions of it.

item.title: Re: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn
item.content:encoded:
The narrative arc regarding the creature's struggle have been added for more well-defined structure.
Other more inconsequential features and facts were removed from Central Narrative for brevity, though likely will be implemented later if this reaches the drafting phase. Hook has also been changed to emphasize how finding purpose doesn't necessarily entail a positive experience in the future.

item.title: Re: Seeking Review for Greenlight
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I've completely revamped the central narrative to shift more focus towards the subject's personal perspective and experience. I also removed the "sleeping giant" trope altogether, with the Frequency now purely interested in gaining knowledge from the many observers it's dispatched.

item.title: Re: Seeking Review for Greenlight
item.content:encoded:
This is conceptually interesting, and I’d definitely wanna fine tune the concept of the anomaly but first.

You’re saying this is a narrative SCP but I’m failing to see the narrative arc. Spell it out, not the mystery, but the logistics of the story.

Also, characterization would be key here as otherwise the reader won’t much care that this being is facing an existential dread.

Additionally, think this would be better without the doom of the Frequency’s awakening. It distracts from the existential horror to some extent… lots of eldritch beings on the wiki that are Big Bad Cthulhu types… but what if it didn’t have an agenda except knowledge? Focusing instead of the experience of the pawn

item.title: Re: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Howdy hey author,

Rather than posting a new comment, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment.

Thank you.

item.title: Re: Seeking Review for Greenlight
item.content:encoded: Latest version moved to top of thread.

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
is it possible to retry this on the same thread, or do I need to make an entirely new thread?

Per the Ideas Critique forum required reading: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas please do not create multiple threads for the same working concept. Just continue this thread. :)

item.title: Re: Seeking Review for Greenlight
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the feedback. I wasn't sure how far to tip my hat for the rest of the details or what other work I needed to fix, but I'll definitely get on that.

Also a quick question for anyone willing to answer, is it possible to retry this on the same thread, or do I need to make an entirely new thread?

item.title: Re: Seeking Review for Greenlight
item.content:encoded:
Heya, I'll stop by and share my thoughts.

+Let's do it!
-Yeet

Amnesiac humanoid emissary from another dimension arrives with existential dread of own worthlessness in a greater plan unbeknownst to anyone.

If it's amnesiac, how does it feel dread of some greater plan? With wording like that, it shouldn't even have knowledge of this greater plan.

It is one of many that exist, though never in the same universe.

Going by the multiverse theory, every alternate universe (or at least most) would/should already have a different version of every person, so this really shouldn't be that unique.

This narrative is not about the creature; the creature is the side character to something else’s story.

This part interests me but is never expanded upon. Although, 'pawn to bigger plan' stories aren't exactly unique, but a higher being using every multiverse version of one person for a greater plan certainly is unique, but that's never properly explored or explained here.

however they exist solely to accomplish some task for some master plan they remain unaware of, or just to die.

Sorry to repeat myself so often, but please tell us what this plan is.

the creature is self-aware to be unimportant and expendable. .

Again, with amnesia, how does it know this?

Also, this may just be a personal thing, but I greatly dislike amnesia as a plot device. It always feels like such a lazy way to create mystery. I feel like nearly every amnesia-based plot device would be much stronger if the entities/characters just..withheld information?

'I can't remember my backstory but I conveniently know how everything works' ceases to interest me.

Only hint to past is anomalous staticky screams it deems familiar made audible whenever near electronic devices.

Also not explored in this pitch.

It is considered of extradimensional origin, but it is incapable of extradimensional travel.

Because something put it in every universe, it didn't go there on its own.

All in all, I can feel a grand, underlying plot and a desire to see it through, but there's very little given here to make heads nor tail of it.

Apologies of this was harsh, but every detail here will need significant expansion if you want to garner interest.

I hope I helped and I bid you a lovely day!

👍👍👍

item.title: {Seeking Review for Greenlight}
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes
Page Type: SCP Article
Genre: Weird, Existential

Elevator Pitch: Humanoid emissary from another dimension arrives with existential dread of own worthlessness in a greater plan to gather data on the multiverse. It is one of many that exist, though none share the same appearance or destiny in the same universe. Each remembers the sentient entity called the “Frequency” who brought them there, which can transmute sound waves into matter (and vice-versa); even capable of compressing and accelerating sound waves to transcend dimensional boundaries. The Frequency has also grown interested in our dimension.

+ Read More
- Hide Section

Central Narrative: Subject was sent extradimensionally to complete its goal of understanding our universe by will of a greater entity known only as “The Frequency”. The Frequency is a sentient, sound-based entity that seeks greater knowledge of the multiverse, dispersing various humanoid-like figures across timelines, each with specific missions and purposes. Both mental and physiological limitations instilled in each one by their creator are intended to control their decisions to minimize potential rogue behaviors. If any of these individuals outright refuse orders, the Frequency will remotely terminate them and create another to fill its role.
The focus of this SCP article is emphasizing the emotional struggle one such subject, and how they are abundantly aware of their own worthlessness and inescapable fate. The narrative arc will be expressed through extensive documentation and conducted interviews with the humanoid detailing how the experience has damaged the subject both mentally and emotionally, feeling empty and hollow from the burden of their intended purpose. It knows it is expected to die someday like all the others, considered no more valuable in the eyes of the Frequency than how the SCP Foundation values D-Class personnel. Despite this, the entity cannot bring itself to turn on its creator, with desperate hope of a slim possibility it may live to see the Frequency’s plans through. These hopes, unfortunately, fall on deaf ears.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: With this narrative-driven SCP article, an emissary from another world is not empowered by its defined sense of purpose, rather burdened by it instead.

Tags: acoustic, extradimensional, euclid, humanoid, knowledge, language, sapient, sentient
